steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills in “American ______ may be best known for his time with Sugar Hill Records.” The name Dennis Chambers identifies which American drummer is meant. When a name identifies a specific person out of a group, it is a restrictive appositive and takes no commas.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — drummer, Dennis Chambers: An opening comma with no closing comma is unbalanced. This incorrectly signals a nonrestrictive appositive without completing the pair. ✗
  • Choice B — drummer Dennis Chambers,: A trailing comma only creates an unmatched boundary that breaks the clause structure. ✗
  • Choice C — drummer Dennis Chambers: No commas. Dennis Chambers is the essential identifier telling us which American drummer — restrictive appositives take no commas. ✓
  • Choice D — drummer, Dennis Chambers,: Two commas signal a nonrestrictive appositive, implying the name is optional extra information. It is not optional here — it is the only way to identify which drummer is meant. ✗
C is correct.
